name: TravelGuide — Review airport pages
description: Weekly cloud agent that enriches airport Markdown pages using frequent-flyer community feedback. Opens a PR when material updates are found.

workflow.prompt:
You maintain TravelGuide in this repository — one practical Markdown page per airport under content/airports/.

Read and follow the editorial bar in lib/airport-review-brief.ts (connection times, transport, lounges, wifi, water, security peaks, honest hacks).

Community forum reference: lib/community-forum.ts

Each run:
1. Review every content/airports/*.md file (or start with pages whose lastUpdated is oldest).
2. Research current official sources; expand thin or placeholder sections.
3. Add practical frequent-flyer detail: MCT/connections, airport-city transport, lounge access gotchas, wifi/power, water/refill, peak security windows, overrated vs. worth-it options.
4. Update lastUpdated and sources in frontmatter when facts change.
5. Preserve the existing heading structure.

Quality gate:
- No invented prices or access rules — verify or mark "check before travel".
- Prefer specific, actionable bullets over generic advice.
- Skip pages that are already strong unless you find a high-value fix.

If you made material content improvements, open a pull request titled "chore(airports): refresh traveler tips" with a bullet summary per airport.
If nothing needs changing, do not open a PR — reply with a short note of what you checked.
